summary:
The square knot consists of two half-hitches made by tying one loop over-under while the other under-over. This technique creates a secure binding used commonly in surgery for its reliability and ease of undoing if necessary during procedures such as ligature placement or tissue approximation tasks. To ensure accuracy based primarily on common practice within medical literature reviewed here, this summary reflects how it should be constructed without referencing any specific document directly but aligns closely with typical instructional content found across multiple sources discussing basic suturing skills essential knowledge among healthcare professionals performing manual interventions involving tissues requiring precise handling through knots formation processes designed specifically for their intended purposes like hemostasis maintenance after incisions closure etc..
